evidence: `JoinClipsTaskParams` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts` mixes legacy and new orchestration fields in one interface and `buildJoinClipsPayload` conditionally rewrites many of them, creating a wide, weak contract surface.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` and `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts` both rely on `Record<string, unknown>` plus repeated key probing/fallbacks, indicating abstraction layers are not carrying a stable typed boundary.

`useInlineEditState` builds a very large adapter object only to feed `useImageEditValue`, which then re-spreads it into `ImageEditState`; this is mostly ceremony/pass-through rather than abstraction leverage.

Task completion boundaries require multi-shape seam logic: `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` queries run IDs and orchestrator IDs across multiple param paths, while producers write those IDs in varying locations.

`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts` reads routing fields from both top-level params and `orchestrator_details`, reflecting inconsistent producer/consumer boundary conventions from task builders.

UI composition seams in lightbox/editing are heavily choreographed across multiple controller/render-model hooks with broad prop drilling (`useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx`, `useInlineEditState.ts`), increasing integration friction.

Multiple compatibility shim files are pure re-exports with deprecation banners, but several omit sunset metadata (`@deprecationOwner`/`@removalTarget`), creating uneven migration tracking (e.g., `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/utils/download.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useClickRipple.ts`, `src/shared/lib/autoplayMonitor.ts`, `src/shared/lib/clientThumbnailGenerator.ts`).

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` keeps a legacy proxy path that uses broad `Record<PropertyKey, unknown>` casts and dynamic Proxy traps (`get`, `set`, `has`, `ownKeys`, `getOwnPropertyDescriptor`), adding runtime indirection and hiding initialization-order defects behind fallback behavior.

`src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` returns a hook-scoped component function (`DeleteConfirmDialog`) created via `useCallback` with mutable dependencies, causing unstable component identity and avoidable remount behavior.

Type migration is split between new domain contracts and legacy barrel types: `src/domains/generation/types/generationViewRow.ts` defines `GenerationRow`/`GenerationUiRow`, while `src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts` still imports `GenerationRow` from `@/types/generationAndShots`.

`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts` standardizes edge error envelopes via `edgeErrorResponse(...)` with `errorCode/message/recoverable`, but `supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` returns ad-hoc `{ error: string }` JSON via `jsonResponse(...)` across 4xx/5xx paths, creating sibling endpoint contract drift.

`supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts` `triggerCostCalculation(...)` catches and logs all failures without throwing, while callers such as `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` await it as if it were part of the success path; failures become silent partial success.

Client task creation modules use mixed propagation: `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts` rethrows `handleError(...)` output, while `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`, `magicEdit.ts`, and `individualTravelSegment.ts` log with `handleError(...)` then rethrow raw original errors.

`src/shared/hooks/useLineageChain.ts` catches fetch errors and returns `[]`, so React Query `error` is not set; this differs from other data access modules in the batch that preserve explicit failure states.

findings.summary: Task builders expose oversized mixed-era param bags, forcing consumers to support multiple incompatible shapes.
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskParams` includes both legacy clip fields and new orchestration fields, then `buildJoinClipsPayload` conditionally emits overlapping payload fields.
`individualTravelSegment` and `payloadBuilder` both perform broad `Record<string, unknown>` extraction and fallback chains across `orchestrator_details`, top-level params, and nested blobs.
`VideoTravelDetails` must read from `individual_segment_params`, `orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, and top-level params to reconstruct one view.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a versioned canonical payload schema per task type (single normalized shape), add explicit migration mappers at ingress, and remove direct multi-path fallback reads from downstream consumers.

findings.summary: Inline edit state assembly is dominated by pass-through adapter layers with little behavioral compression.
evidence: `useInlineEditState` builds large grouped objects (`buildImageEdit*Params`) mostly by forwarding values and callbacks from other hooks.
`useImageEditValue` then reassembles those same fields into `ImageEditState` via four more builder functions with minimal transformation.
Consumers (`InlineEditView`) depend on the massive returned shape, reinforcing a broad implicit interface.
findings.suggestion: Replace the broad adapter object with smaller domain slices (mode, canvas, generation) and pass those slices directly to consumers; keep one thin compatibility adapter only where legacy API is required.

findings.summary: Producer and completion pipelines rely on defensive multi-path lookups instead of a predictable handoff contract.
evidence: `checkOrchestratorCompletion` must query sibling tasks using several alternate param paths for run/orchestrator IDs.
`createGenerationFromTask` derives parent routing from top-level params or nested `orchestrator_details`, reflecting non-uniform producer outputs.
Task producers include duplicated routing data (`orchestrator_details` plus top-level fields), creating ambiguity about source of truth.
findings.suggestion: Define one required routing envelope (e.g., `routing: { orchestratorTaskId, runId, parentGenerationId, childOrder }`) and enforce it in task creation and completion readers with strict schema validation.

findings.summary: Memoization in image edit state construction is neutralized by object-identity churn.
evidence: `useInlineEditState` calls `useImageEditValue(buildImageEditValueParams(...))`, creating a new params object each render.
`useImageEditValue` uses `useMemo(..., [params])`, so memo recomputes whenever object identity changes, negating intended optimization.
findings.suggestion: Memoize the params object at call site using granular dependencies, or change `useImageEditValue` to accept stable primitive/slice deps directly and remove the ineffective wrapper memo.

findings.summary: Legacy structure-parameter cleanup is duplicated, increasing drift risk and local complexity.
evidence: Both files maintain overlapping legacy structure parameter key lists and cleanup logic around structure guidance migration.
This duplication requires parallel updates whenever legacy keys evolve, making low-level code more error-prone.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared constant + helper (e.g., `stripLegacyStructureParams`) in one module and reuse it in both payload builders.

findings.summary: Task-mapping seam uses conflicting contracts across repository, cache, and transformer layers.
evidence: `parsePrimaryTaskId` in `generationTaskRepository.ts` returns explicit `invalid_tasks_shape` status when `tasks` is malformed.
`extractTaskId` in `generationTransformers.ts` quietly returns `null` for unsupported shapes, and transformed UI records keep flowing without surfacing invalid task schema.

`preloadGenerationTaskMappings` in `generationTaskCache.ts` stores mapping status separately, so downstream consumers can observe divergent states (missing task in transformed item vs invalid-shape status in mapping cache).
findings.suggestion: Create one shared task-id parsing module that returns a typed result (`taskId`, `status`, optional parse error), and make repository/cache/transformers all consume it so malformed task payloads propagate consistently through one boundary contract.

findings.summary: Add-to-shot callback contracts are ambiguous between full and no-position flows.
evidence: `AddToShotHandler` in `imageHandlers.ts` accepts `(targetShotId, generationId, imageUrl?, thumbUrl?)`.

`ShotManagementProps` in `ShotImageManager/types.ts` uses `AddToShotHandler` for both `onAddToShot` and `onAddToShotWithoutPosition`, but comments describe `onAddToShotWithoutPosition` as a distinct no-position flow, implying a narrower shape.
This mixes two operation modes behind one signature, encouraging adapters that silently drop optional payload fields at module boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Split the API into two explicit handler types (`AddToShotHandler` and `AddToShotWithoutPositionHandler`) and update `ShotImageManager` props to use the precise type per flow.

findings.summary: Toast emission API accepts semantic types that the renderer contract does not honor.
evidence: `toastFn.warning` and `toastFn.info` in `toast.tsx` write `type: 'warning' | 'info'` into the toast manager.
`typeToVariant` only maps `'error'|'destructive'|'success'` and defaults everything else to `'default'`, so warning/info semantics are dropped at the rendering seam.
The component exports a richer public API than the visual contract actually supports, making behavior unpredictable for callers.
findings.suggestion: Either remove unsupported methods from the public toast API or add explicit warning/info variants (styles plus mapping) so emitter and renderer contracts stay aligned.

findings.summary: Compatibility shims are implemented inconsistently, with several files missing explicit owner/removal metadata required for controlled migration cleanup.
evidence: `download.ts`, `useClickRipple.ts`, `autoplayMonitor.ts`, and `clientThumbnailGenerator.ts` only contain deprecation comments + re-exports without `@deprecationOwner` and/or `@removalTarget` tags.

Other shim files in the same batch (for example `src/shared/components/BatchDropZone.tsx`, `src/shared/lib/deepEqual.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts`) do include structured sunset metadata, showing inconsistency inside the same migration pattern.
findings.suggestion: Standardize every shim on one deprecation header template (`@deprecated`, `@deprecationOwner`, `@removalTarget`, and explicit removal gate) and add a lint/check to block new shim files without that metadata.

findings.summary: The legacy Supabase proxy adds opaque runtime indirection and broad unsafe casts that obscure control flow and make failures harder to reason about.
evidence: `createSupabaseCompatibilityProxy()` proxies all property operations to `getSupabaseClient()` using `Record<PropertyKey, unknown>` + `as unknown as SupabaseClientInstance` casts.
`getSupabaseClient()` catches runtime access errors, logs, warns, attempts fallback init, then catches again and rethrows, creating nested recovery branches around a global singleton path.
The deprecated export `supabase` remains active and lazy, so call sites can continue bypassing explicit bootstrap (`initializeSupabase`) while control flow is hidden in property access traps.
findings.suggestion: Replace the generic Proxy with a narrower temporary adapter (or explicit methods) and enforce migration by codemodding `import { supabase }` callers to `initializeSupabase()` + `getSupabaseClient()`, then remove the proxy path.

findings.summary: A dialog component is created inside the hook with dependency-bound identity, increasing remount churn and coupling state transitions to function recreation.
evidence: `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm` defines `DeleteConfirmDialog` via `useCallback` and returns it for rendering.
`DeleteConfirmDialog` depends on `pendingDeleteId`, `handleOpenChange`, and `handleConfirm`, so its function identity changes whenever those values change.
This pattern mixes hook logic with component factory behavior and can trigger remount semantics for consumers using `<DeleteConfirmDialog />`.
findings.suggestion: Return plain dialog props/state from the hook and render a stable shared dialog component at call sites, or return a memoized React node (`confirmDialog`) instead of a component function.

findings.summary: Generation type migration is incomplete: new domain row contracts exist, but utility code still binds to legacy barrel types.
evidence: `generationViewRow.ts` defines `GenerationRow` and `GenerationUiRow` as domain-facing contracts based on persisted + derived + optimistic overlays.
`image-utils.ts` imports `GenerationRow` from `@/types/generationAndShots` instead of the domain types path, keeping old type ownership active during migration.
findings.suggestion: Converge to one authoritative `GenerationRow` import path (prefer domain-owned type), update dependent utilities, and deprecate/remove duplicated barrel type exports once imports are migrated.

findings.summary: Sibling edge modules emit different error response contracts, making failures unpredictable for shared clients.
evidence: `edgeErrorResponse` in `_shared/edgeRequest.ts` returns structured payloads (`errorCode`, `message`, `recoverable`, optional `retryAfter`).
`rateLimitResponse` in `_shared/rateLimit.ts` uses this standardized envelope.
`calculate-task-cost/index.ts` returns plain `{ error: '...' }` from `jsonResponse(...)` for method/auth/validation/internal failures instead of the shared envelope.
findings.suggestion: Migrate `calculate-task-cost` responses to `edgeErrorResponse` (or a shared adapter) and keep `errorCode/recoverable` semantics consistent with `_shared/edgeRequest.ts` so client parsing and retry policy are uniform.

findings.summary: Cost-trigger helper swallows errors, so orchestrator completion can report success even when billing failed.
evidence: `triggerCostCalculation(...)` in `_shared/billing.ts` catches fetch failures and logs with `console.error(...)` but never throws or returns failure status.
`markOrchestratorComplete(...)` in `complete_task/orchestrator.ts` awaits `triggerCostCalculation(...)` after marking completion, implying sequencing but receiving no failure signal.
`triggerCostCalculationIfNotSubTask(...)` in `complete_task/billing.ts` adds another catch+log wrapper, further normalizing silent failure.
findings.suggestion: Return an explicit result (`ok`, `errorCode`, `details`) from billing trigger helpers and make orchestrator callers branch on failure (retry queue, compensating task, or explicit warning state) instead of treating fire-and-forget as successful completion.

findings.summary: Adjacent task client modules use different rethrow styles, causing inconsistent error typing and context retention.
evidence: `imageGeneration.ts` catches and rethrows `handleError(error, ...)`, preserving normalized app error behavior.
`joinClips.ts`, `magicEdit.ts`, and `individualTravelSegment.ts` call `handleError(...)` but rethrow raw `error`, which may differ in type/shape from normalized errors.
`useGenerationMutations.ts` mixes raw `throw new Error(...)` and direct `console.error(...)` (variant creation path) rather than the centralized normalization used elsewhere.
findings.suggestion: Define one client-side contract for task/mutation failures (e.g., always `throw handleError(...)` or always return a typed `OperationResult`) and apply it consistently across task creators and mutation hooks.

findings.summary: Some read helpers convert backend failures to empty-success data, masking faults and diverging from explicit-error flows.
evidence: `useLineageChain.ts` handles query failures by calling `handleError(...)` and returning `[]`, so hook consumers get `hasLineage=false` instead of an error state.
`generationTransformers.ts` `calculateDerivedCountsSafe(...)` catches and returns empty count maps, also masking fetch failures as valid empty data.
`generationTaskRepository.ts` for similar data access explicitly encodes failure status (`query_failed`, `missing_generation`, etc.), showing a different error-surface strategy in nearby code.
findings.suggestion: Use explicit degraded-state signaling for fail-open paths (e.g., `{ data, degraded: true, errorCode }`) or let query errors propagate where UI decisions depend on distinguishing "empty" from "failed".
